channel.title: Go on Ezra Natanael
channel.description: Recent content in Go on Ezra Natanael

item.title: Understanding Pointer in Go
item.description: Introduction Pointers are fundamental to Go programming, serving as a way to directly work with memory addresses. While they might seem intimidating at first, understanding pointers is crucial for writing efficient Go code. This guide balances theory and practice to help you master pointer concepts. What is a Pointer? A pointer is a variable that stores the memory address of another variable. Think of it like a house address - instead of containing the house itself, it contains directions to find the house.
